---
title: "Getting Started"
page-layout: full
---

::: {.hero}
::: {.container}
# Getting Started

This page is the fastest path from "I installed the package" to "I know where everything is." DPmixGPD has a lot of surface area; the goal here is to make the first 10 minutes frictionless.

[Open the Examples](Examples/index.html){.btn .btn-primary}
[Decision Guide (next)](decision-guide.html){.btn .btn-outline-light}
:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

::: {.grid-3}

::: {.cardx}
### 1) Install and verify
Once the package installs cleanly, most problems become modeling decisions rather than setup decisions. If you hit build issues, use the Developers page for the reproducibility checklist.
- [Developers](developers.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 2) Pick the right entry point
- **Examples**: end-to-end workflows and recommended sequence.
- **Kernels**: supported likelihood families and tail combinations.
- **Reference**: curated door into the full API.
- [Examples](Examples/index.html)
- [Kernels](kernels.html)
- [Reference](reference.html)
:::

::: {.cardx}
### 3) Two decisions that matter early
Most early choices reduce to:
- **Bulk kernel** (what matches the typical range of your data)
- **Tail modeling** (whether a GPD tail is needed and where the threshold sits)

If you want guided selection, use the Decision Guide.
:::

:::

:::
:::

::: {.section}
::: {.container}

## Where the detailed documentation lives
This Quarto wrapper keeps the "map" pages readable. The automatically generated documentation lives under pkgdown:
- [Pkgdown home](/pkgdown/index.html)
- [API index](pkgdown/reference/index.html)
- [Articles index](pkgdown/articles/index.html)

## Recommended next steps
1. Open the Examples and follow the first workflow end-to-end.
2. Use Kernels to confirm the distributional family you want for the bulk.
3. Use Reference to jump to function details once you recognize the relevant function name.

:::
:::
